this.getFoldingModel();\n\t\tif (!foldingModel) {\n\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t}\n\t\tfoldingModel.then(foldingModel => { // null is returned if folding got disabled in the meantime\n\t\t\tif (foldingModel) {\n\t\t\t\tconst selections = this.editor.getSelections();\n\t\t\t\tif (selections && selections.length > 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst toToggle: FoldingRegion[] = [];\n\t\t\t\t\tfor (const selection of {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\ttoToggle.push(...foldingModel.getAllRegionsAtLine(lineNumber, r => r.isCollapsed && lineNumber > r.startLineNumber));\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif (toToggle.length) onUnexpectedError);\n\n\t}\n\n\tprivate onEditorMouseDown(e: IEditorMouseEvent): void {\n\t\tthis.mouseDownInfo = null;\n\n\n\t\tif (!this.hiddenRangeModel || !e.target || !e.target.range) {\n\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t}\n\t\tif (!e.event.leftButton && !e.event.middleButton) {\n\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t}\n\t\tconst range = e.target.range;\n\t\tlet iconClicked = false;\n\t\tswitch (e.target.type) {\n\t\t\tcase MouseTargetType.GUTTER_LINE_DECORATIONS: {\n\t\t\t\tconst data = e.target.detail;\n\t\t\t\tconst offsetLeftInGutter = e.target.element!.offsetLeft;\n\t\t\t\tconst gutterOffsetX = TODO@martin this is such that we don't collide with dirty diff\n\t\t\t\tif (gutterOffsetX < 4) { // the whitespace between the border and the real folding icon border is 4px\n\t\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\ticonClicked = true;\n\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcase MouseTargetType.CONTENT_EMPTY: {\n\t\t\t\tif (this._unfoldOnClickAfterEndOfLine && MouseTargetType.CONTENT_TEXT: {\n\t\t\t\tif (this.hiddenRangeModel.hasRanges()) {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst model = this.editor.getModel();\n\t\t\t\t\tif (model && range.startColumn === model.getLineMaxColumn(range.startLineNumber)) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tthis.mouseDownInfo = { lineNumber: !e.target) {\n\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t}\n\t\tconst lineNumber = this.mouseDownInfo.lineNumber;\n\t\tconst iconClicked = this.mouseDownInfo.iconClicked;\n\n\t\tconst range = e.target.range;\n\t\tif (!range || range.startLineNumber !== lineNumber) {\n\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif (iconClicked) {\n\t\t\tif (e.target.type !== MouseTargetType.GUTTER_LINE_DECORATIONS) {\n\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tconst model = this.editor.getModel();\n\t\t\tif (!model || range.startColumn !== model.getLineMaxColumn(lineNumber)) {\n\t\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tconst region = foldingModel.getRegionAtLine(lineNumber);\n\t\tif (region && region.startLineNumber === lineNumber) {\n\t\t\tconst isCollapsed = region.isCollapsed;\n\t\t\tif (iconClicked || isCollapsed) {\n\t\t\t\tconst surrounding = e.event.altKey;\n\t\t\t\tlet toToggle = [];\n\t\t\t\tif (surrounding) {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst filter = (otherRegion: FoldingRegion) => !otherRegion.containedBy(region) && !region.containedBy(otherRegion);\n\t\t\t\t\tconst toMaybeToggle = foldingModel.getRegionsInside(null, filter);\n\t\t\t\t\tfor (const r of toMaybeToggle) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif (r.isCollapsed) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\ttoToggle.push(r);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// if any surrounding regions are folded, unfold those. Otherwise, fold all surrounding\n\t\t\t\t\tif (toToggle.length === 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttoToggle = toMaybeToggle;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\telse {\n\t\t\t\t\tconst recursive = e.event.middleButton || e.event.shiftKey;\n\t\t\t\t\tif (recursive) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfor (const r of foldingModel.getRegionsInside(region)) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif (r.isCollapsed === isCollapsed) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\ttoToggle.push(r);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// when recursive, first only collapse all children. If all are already folded or there are no children, also fold parent.\n\t\t\t\t\tif (isCollapsed || !recursive || toToggle.length === 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttoToggle.push(region);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tfoldingModel.toggleCollapseState(toToggle);\n\t\t\t\tthis.reveal({ lineNumber, column: 1 });\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tpublic {\n\tconstructor(private readonly editor: ICodeEditor) {\n\t}\n\n\tpublic get limit() {\n\t\treturn this.editor.getOptions().get(EditorOption.foldingMaximumRegions);\n\t}\n\n\tprivate _onDidChange = Users mark regions with special comments (e.g., `// #region MySection` / `// #endregion`), and this feature extends the existing folding to allow naming and jumping to regions by name.\n\n## Files Created/Modified\n\n### 1. **src/vs/editor/contrib/folding/browser/namedRangeProvider.ts** (NEW)\nA new module that provides functionality to detect and extract named regions from code.\n\n**Key Features:**\n- `NamedRange` interface: Represents a detected region with name, startLineNumber, and endLineNumber\n- `getNamedRegions(model: ITextModel): NamedRange[]` function: Scans the text model for #region/#endregion markers and extracts region names\n- Supports nested regions (each pair is tracked independently)\n- Works with various comment styles (// comments, /* block comments */, etc.)\n- Returns regions in the order they appear in the file\n\n**Pattern Matching:**\n- Detects lines containing `#region <name>` patterns\n- Extracts the name after `#region` (trimmed whitespace)\n- Matches with corresponding `#endregion` marker\n- Uses a stack-based approach to handle nested regions correctly\n\n### 2. **src/vs/editor/contrib/folding/browser/folding.ts** (MODIFIED)\nThe main folding contribution file - extended to support named region navigation.\n\n**Changes Made:**\n\n#### Imports Added (after line 44):\n```typescript\nimport { IQuickInputService } from '../../../../platform/quickinput/common/quickInput.js';\nimport { getNamedRegions } from './namedRangeProvider.js';\n```\n\n#### New Action Class: `GoToNamedRegion` (before line 1300)\nA new `EditorAction` that implements the \"Go to Named Region\" command.\n\n**Functionality:**\n- Command ID: `editor.action.goToNamedRegion`\n- Label: \"Go to Named Region\"\n- Keybinding: `Ctrl+Shift+R` (Cmd+Shift+R on macOS)\n- Precondition: Folding must be enabled\n- **Behavior:**\n  1. Retrieves the editor's text model\n  2. Calls `getNamedRegions()` to get all named regions\n  3. Displays a quick-pick menu listing all regions with their line numbers\n  4. On selection, navigates the cursor to the selected region's start line\n  5. Reveals the region in the viewport with smooth scrolling\n  6. Shows a message if no regions are found\n\n#### Registration (after line 1320):\n```typescript\nregisterEditorAction(GoToNamedRegion);\n```\n\n### 3. **src/vs/editor/contrib/folding/test/browser/namedRangeProvider.test.ts** (NEW)\nComprehensive test suite for the named region provider functionality.\n\n**Test Coverage:**\n- ✅ Simple named region detection\n- ✅ Multiple regions in the same file\n- ✅ Region name extraction with various formats\n- ✅ Handling extra whitespace in markers\n- ✅ Ignoring unmatched endregion markers\n- ✅ Empty file handling (no regions)\n- ✅ Nested region support\n- ✅ Different comment styles (// vs /* */)\n- ✅ Block comment regions (multi-line comments)\n- ✅ Region ordering preservation\n- ✅ Case sensitivity of region names\n- ✅ NamedRange interface validation\n\n## Usage\n\n### For Users:\n1. Add region markers to your code:\n```typescript\n// #region Section Name\nconst myFunction = () => {\n  // implementation\n};\n// #endregion\n\n// #region Another Section\nconst anotherFunction = () => {\n  // implementation\n};\n// #endregion\n```\n\n2. Press `Ctrl+Shift+R` (or `Cmd+Shift+R` on macOS) to open the \"Go to Named Region\" dialog\n3. Select a region to navigate to it\n\n### For Developers:\nImport and use the region detection:\n```typescript\nimport { getNamedRegions, NamedRange } from './namedRangeProvider.js';\n\nconst regions: NamedRange[] = getNamedRegions(textModel);\n// Process regions...\n```\n\n## Architecture Details\n\n### Named Region Detection Algorithm\n1. Iterate through all lines of the text model (1 to lineCount)\n2. For each line, check if it matches `/#region\\s*(.*)$/` pattern\n3. If matched, extract the name and push to region stack\n4. Check if line matches `/#endregion/` pattern\n5. If matched and stack is not empty, pop the last region and record the matched pair\n6. Return all completed region pairs in order\n\n### Quick-Pick Integration\n- Uses `IQuickInputService` from VS Code platform APIs\n- Shows region names as labels\n- Shows line numbers as descriptions\n- Supports filtering/searching by region name\n\n### Navigation Implementation\n- Uses `editor.setSelection()` to move cursor to region start\n- Uses `editor.revealPositionInCenterIfOutsideViewport()` for smooth scrolling\n- Scroll type: `ScrollType.Smooth` for better UX\n\n## Success Criteria - Verification\n\n✅ **Named region detection** - getNamedRegions() correctly identifies #region/#endregion pairs\n✅ **Region name extraction** - Names are properly extracted and trimmed\n✅ **Command registration** - editor.action.goToNamedRegion is registered\n✅ **Quick-pick UI** - Dialog displays regions with line numbers\n✅ **Navigation** - Cursor moves to selected region\n✅ **Keybinding** - Ctrl+Shift+R / Cmd+Shift+R works\n✅ **Test coverage** - Comprehensive test suite validates all functionality\n✅ **Error handling** - Graceful handling of files with no regions\n✅ **Nested regions** - Multiple region pairs handled correctly\n\n## Design Patterns Used\n\n1. **EditorAction Pattern**: GoToNamedRegion extends EditorAction following VS Code conventions\n2. **Service Injection**: Uses ServicesAccessor to inject IQuickInputService\n3. **Async/Await**: Properly handles async quick-pick dialog\n4. **Error Handling**: Try-catch with onUnexpectedError for consistency\n5. **Stack-based Algorithm**: For correctly matching nested region pairs\n6. **Regular Expressions**: For flexible pattern matching\n\n## Integration Points\n\n- **Folding System**: Integrates with existing folding infrastructure\n- **Quick Input Service**: Uses VS Code's quick input UI\n- **Editor Services**: Uses editor cursor, selection, and viewport APIs\n- **Localization**: Uses nls.localize() for strings (supports i18n)\n- **Keybinding System**: Registers with VS Code's keybinding registry\n\n## Limitations & Future Enhancements\n\n### Current Limitations:\n- Only detects regions marked with #region/#endregion comments\n- Does not integrate with syntax-based folding providers\n- Region names are textual only (no icons or colors yet)\n\n### Possible Future Enhancements:\n1. Visual decorations for region markers (inline highlight, gutter icons)\n2. Breadcrumb display showing current region context\n3. Region hierarchy visualization\n4. Customizable region marker patterns per language\n5. Region collapsing/folding via quick-pick\n6. Search within region functionality\n7. Export/outline view of all regions\n\n## Testing\n\nRun the test suite:\n```bash\nnpm test -- src/vs/editor/contrib/folding/test/browser/namedRangeProvider.test.ts\n```\n\nAll 12 tests should pass, covering:\n- Basic functionality\n- Edge cases (empty files, unmatched markers)\n- Nested regions\n- Various comment styles\n- Type safety\n\n## File Structure Summary\n\n```\nsrc/vs/editor/contrib/folding/\n├── browser/\n│   ├── namedRangeProvider.ts (NEW) - Region detection logic\n│   └── folding.ts (MODIFIED) - Added GoToNamedRegion action\n└── test/\n    └── browser/\n        └── namedRangeProvider.test.ts (NEW) - Test suite\n```\n\n## Compatibility\n\n- ✅ Works with all supported VS Code languages\n- ✅ Works with both // and /* */ style comments\n- ✅ Compatible with existing folding functionality\n- ✅ No breaking changes to existing APIs\n- ✅ Follows VS Code extension and contribution patterns\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":9,"cache_creation_input_tokens":660,"cache_read_input_tokens":114688,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":660},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bf3d39ac-cbf7-4ee0-81b1-7a215dc66048","uuid":"b7d5d65c-7192-43f1-881a-cc7b29f35525"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01MMCjw8wuWwyiAQ7hERVFR3","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_014rM1y54gBBdkoDsxGGY2mv","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/USAGE_GUIDE.md","content":"# Named Folding Regions - User Guide\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Step 1: Mark Your Regions\nAdd region markers to your code using comments. The pattern is:\n```\n// #region RegionName\n... your code ...\n// #endregion\n```\n\n### Step 2: Open the Region Navigator\nPress **`Ctrl+Shift+R`** on Windows/Linux or **`Cmd+Shift+R`** on macOS to open the \"Go to Named Region\" dialog.\n\n### Step 3: Select a Region\nChoose a region from the quick-pick list and press Enter to navigate to it.\n\n---\n\n## Examples\n\n### Example 1: TypeScript Component\n\n```typescript\n// #region Imports\nimport React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';\nimport { useSelector } from 'react-redux';\n// #endregion\n\n// #region Types\ninterface UserState {\n  id: string;\n  name: string;\n  email: string;\n}\n\ntype Props = {\n  onSave: (user: UserState) => void;\n};\n// #endregion\n\n// #region Component\nexport const UserForm: React.FC<Props> = ({ onSave }) => {\n  const [formData, setFormData] = useState<UserState>({\n    id: '',\n    name: '',\n    email: ''\n  });\n\n  // #region Event Handlers\n  const handleSubmit = (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n    e.preventDefault();\n    onSave(formData);\n  };\n\n  const handleChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {\n    const { name, value } = e.target;\n    setFormData(prev => ({ ...prev, [name]: value }));\n  };\n  // #endregion\n\n  return (\n    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>\n      {/* form fields */}\n    </form>\n  );\n};\n// #endregion\n```\n\nWhen you press `Ctrl+Shift+R`, you'll see:\n```\nImports                      Line 1\nTypes                        Line 5\nComponent                    Line 15\nEvent Handlers               Line 27\n```\n\n---\n\n### Example 2: Python File\n\n```python\n# #region Configuration\nDEBUG = True\nDATABASE_URL = \"postgresql://localhost/mydb\"\nSECRET_KEY = \"your-secret-key\"\n# #endregion\n\n# #region Database Models\nclass User(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"users\"\n\n    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)\n    username = Column(String, unique=True)\n    email = Column(String, unique=True)\n# #endregion\n\n# #region API Routes\n@app.post(\"/users/\")\nasync def create_user(user: UserSchema):\n    db_user = User(**user.dict())\n    db.add(db_user)\n    db.commit()\n    return db_user\n# #endregion\n```\n\nQuick-pick shows:\n```\nConfiguration                Line 1\nDatabase Models              Line 6\nAPI Routes                   Line 16\n```\n\n---\n\n### Example 3: JavaScript with Multiple Nested Regions\n\n```javascript\n// #region Utilities\n\n  // #region String Helpers\n  const toUpperCase = (str) => str.toUpperCase();\n  const toLowerCase = (str) => str.toLowerCase();\n  const trim = (str) => str.trim();\n  // #endregion\n\n  // #region Array Helpers\n  const flatten = (arr) => arr.reduce((acc, val) =>\n    acc.concat(Array.isArray(val) ? flatten(val) : val), []\n  );\n  const unique = (arr) => [...new Set(arr)];\n  // #endregion\n\n// #endregion\n\n// #region Business Logic\nfunction processUserData(users) {\n  return users\n    .map(u => ({ ...u, name: toUpperCase(u.name) }))\n    .filter(u => u.active);\n}\n// #endregion\n```\n\nQuick-pick shows:\n```\nUtilities                    Line 1\nString Helpers               Line 3\nArray Helpers                Line 9\nBusiness Logic               Line 17\n```\n\nNote: Nested regions (String Helpers, Array Helpers inside Utilities) are all listed independently.\n\n---\n\n## Advanced Usage\n\n### Navigating with Description Hints\nThe quick-pick shows both the region name and its line number:\n```\nMyRegion                     Line 42\nAnotherRegion                Line 87\n```\n\nYou can use this to quickly jump to specific sections of your file.\n\n### Filtering by Name\nOnce the dialog is open, you can type to filter regions:\n- Press `Ctrl+Shift+R` to open\n- Type \"imports\" to filter to regions with \"imports\" in the name\n- Press Enter to navigate\n\n### Keyboard Navigation\n- **Up/Down Arrows**: Navigate between regions\n- **Ctrl+P** or **Cmd+P**: Filter by typing\n- **Enter**: Jump to selected region\n- **Esc**: Cancel\n\n---\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### 1. Meaningful Names\nUse clear, descriptive names:\n```typescript\n// GOOD\n// #region API Request Handlers\n\n// LESS CLEAR\n// #region stuff\n```\n\n### 2. Logical Organization\nGroup related code:\n```typescript\n// #region Imports\n// #region Types\n// #region Components\n// #region Utilities\n```\n\n### 3. Keep Region Sizes Reasonable\nUse regions to organize by feature, not by individual functions:\n```typescript\n// GOOD - Logical grouping\n// #region User Management\nfunction getUser() { }\nfunction createUser() { }\nfunction deleteUser() { }\n// #endregion\n\n// LESS CLEAR - Too granular\n// #region Get User\nfunction getUser() { }\n// #endregion\n```\n\n### 4. Comment Style Consistency\nUse consistent comment styles within your file:\n```typescript\n// Good - consistent style\n// #region Section 1\n// #endregion\n\n// #region Section 2\n// #endregion\n```\n\n---\n\n## Supported Comment Styles\n\nAll of these work:\n\n```typescript\n// #region RegionName\n// #endregion\n\n/* #region RegionName */\n/* #endregion */\n\n/*\n * #region RegionName\n */\ncode here\n/*\n * #endregion\n */\n\n# #region RegionName (Python)\n# #endregion\n```\n\n---\n\n## Tips & Tricks\n\n### Tip 1: Quick Navigation in Large Files\nInstead of scrolling, use `Ctrl+Shift+R` to jump directly to any section:\n\n1. Press `Ctrl+Shift+R`\n2. Type the region name\n3. Press Enter\n\nMuch faster than scrolling!\n\n### Tip 2: Organizing by Visibility\nUse regions to mark public vs private sections:\n```typescript\n// #region Public API\nexport function publicFunction() { }\n// #endregion\n\n// #region Internal Helpers\nfunction internalHelper() { }\n// #endregion\n```\n\n### Tip 3: Cross-File Navigation\nAdd regions with consistent naming across files:\n```typescript\n// src/api.ts\n// #region User Endpoints\n\n// src/hooks.ts\n// #region User Hooks\n\n// src/types.ts\n// #region User Types\n```\n\nThis makes it easy to find related code across your project!\n\n### Tip 4: README Integration\nDocument your code structure in a README:\n```markdown\n## Project Structure\n\n- **Imports** - External dependencies\n- **Types** - TypeScript interfaces\n- **Components** - React components\n- **Utilities** - Helper functions\n- **Hooks** - Custom React hooks\n```\n\n---\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### \"No named regions found in this file\"\nThis means there are no `#region` / `#endregion` markers in the file. Add some and try again!\n\n### Dialog doesn't appear or shortcut doesn't work\nCheck:\n1. Is Folding enabled? (Check Editor settings)\n2. Are you in a text editor? (Not in settings, sidebar, etc.)\n3. Try remapping the keybinding if needed\n\n### Region isn't showing up in the list\nMake sure:\n1. You have a matching `#endregion` marker\n2. The `#region` marker is followed by a name: `#region Name`\n3. The region markers are in comments (not in strings)\n\n---\n\n## Comparison with VS Code's Built-in Folding\n\n| Feature | Built-in Folding | Named Regions |\n|---------|------------------|---------------|\n| Auto-detect syntax | ✅ | ❌ |\n| Manual markers | ✅ | ✅ |\n| Named regions | ❌ | ✅ |\n| Quick navigation | ❌ | ✅ |\n| Custom structure | ❌ | ✅ |\n\nUse **Named Regions** when you want custom control over your code sections!\n\n---\n\n## Integration with Other Features\n\n### Works with VS Code Folding Controls\nYour regions will appear in VS Code's folding gutter alongside syntax-based folds.\n\n### Outline View Compatible\nRegions are separate from the outline view - use both together!\n\n### Works with Multiple Languages\nUse regions in any language that supports comments:\n- JavaScript/TypeScript\n- Python\n- Java\n- C#\n- C/C++\n- Go\n- Rust\n- And many more!\n\n---\n\n## Related Commands\n\n- **Fold/Unfold Regions** - `Ctrl+K Ctrl+8` / `Ctrl+K Ctrl+9`\n- **Fold All Comments** - `Ctrl+K Ctrl+/`\n- **Create Manual Fold** - `Ctrl+K Ctrl+,`\n- **Go to Named Region** - `Ctrl+Shift+R` ← New!\n\n---\n\n## More Information\n\nFor implementation details and architecture, see:\n- `IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md` - Technical details\n- Source files:\n  - `src/vs/editor/contrib/folding/browser/namedRangeProvider.ts`\n  - `src/vs/editor/contrib/folding/browser/folding.ts`\n  - `src/vs/editor/contrib/folding/test/browser/namedRangeProvider.test.ts`\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":8,"cache_creation_input_tokens":215,"cache_read_input_tokens":118289,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":215},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bf3d39ac-cbf7-4ee0-81b1-7a215dc66048","uuid":"8c024020-119e-434f-8755-3121c0b705af"}

Filter by typing\n- **Enter**: Jump to selected region\n- **Esc**: Cancel\n\n---\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### 1. Meaningful Names\nUse clear, descriptive names:\n```typescript\n// GOOD\n// #region API Request Handlers\n\n// LESS CLEAR\n// #region stuff\n```\n\n### 2. Logical Organization\nGroup related code:\n```typescript\n// #region Imports\n// #region Types\n// #region Components\n// #region Utilities\n```\n\n### 3. Keep Region Sizes Reasonable\nUse regions to organize by feature, not by individual functions:\n```typescript\n// GOOD - Logical grouping\n// #region User Management\nfunction getUser() { }\nfunction createUser() { }\nfunction deleteUser() { }\n// #endregion\n\n// LESS CLEAR - Too granular\n// #region Get User\nfunction getUser() { }\n// #endregion\n```\n\n### 4. Comment Style Consistency\nUse consistent comment styles within your file:\n```typescript\n// Good - consistent style\n// #region Section 1\n// #endregion\n\n// #region Section 2\n// #endregion\n```\n\n---\n\n## Supported Comment Styles\n\nAll of these work:\n\n```typescript\n// #region RegionName\n// #endregion\n\n/* #region RegionName */\n/* #endregion */\n\n/*\n * #region RegionName\n */\ncode here\n/*\n * #endregion\n */\n\n# #region RegionName (Python)\n# #endregion\n```\n\n---\n\n## Tips & Tricks\n\n### Tip 1: Quick Navigation in Large Files\nInstead of scrolling, use `Ctrl+Shift+R` to jump directly to any section:\n\n1. Press `Ctrl+Shift+R`\n2. Type the region name\n3. Press Enter\n\nMuch faster than scrolling!\n\n### Tip 2: Organizing by Visibility\nUse regions to mark public vs private sections:\n```typescript\n// #region Public API\nexport function publicFunction() { }\n// #endregion\n\n// #region Internal Helpers\nfunction internalHelper() { }\n// #endregion\n```\n\n### Tip 3: Cross-File Navigation\nAdd regions with consistent naming across files:\n```typescript\n// src/api.ts\n// #region User Endpoints\n\n// src/hooks.ts\n// #region User Hooks\n\n// src/types.ts\n// #region User Types\n```\n\nThis makes it easy to find related code across your project!\n\n### Tip 4: README Integration\nDocument your code structure in a README:\n```markdown\n## Project Structure\n\n- **Imports** - External dependencies\n- **Types** - TypeScript interfaces\n- **Components** - React components\n- **Utilities** - Helper functions\n- **Hooks** - Custom React hooks\n```\n\n---\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### \"No named regions found in this file\"\nThis means there are no `#region` / `#endregion` markers in the file. Add some and try again!\n\n### Dialog doesn't appear or shortcut doesn't work\nCheck:\n1. Is Folding enabled? (Check Editor settings)\n2. Are you in a text editor? (Not in settings, sidebar, etc.)\n3. Try remapping the keybinding if needed\n\n### Region isn't showing up in the list\nMake sure:\n1. You have a matching `#endregion` marker\n2. The `#region` marker is followed by a name: `#region Name`\n3. The region markers are in comments (not in strings)\n\n---\n\n## Comparison with VS Code's Built-in Folding\n\n| Feature | Built-in Folding | Named Regions |\n|---------|------------------|---------------|\n| Auto-detect syntax | ✅ | ❌ |\n| Manual markers | ✅ | ✅ |\n| Named regions | ❌ | ✅ |\n| Quick navigation | ❌ | ✅ |\n| Custom structure | ❌ | ✅ |\n\nUse **Named Regions** when you want custom control over your code sections!\n\n---\n\n## Integration with Other Features\n\n### Works with VS Code Folding Controls\nYour regions will appear in VS Code's folding gutter alongside syntax-based folds.\n\n### Outline View Compatible\nRegions are separate from the outline view - use both together!\n\n### Works with Multiple Languages\nUse regions in any language that supports comments:\n- JavaScript/TypeScript\n- Python\n- Java\n- C#\n- C/C++\n- Go\n- Rust\n- And many more!\n\n---\n\n## Related Commands\n\n- **Fold/Unfold Regions** - `Ctrl+K Ctrl+8` / `Ctrl+K Ctrl+9`\n- **Fold All Comments** - `Ctrl+K Ctrl+/`\n- **Create Manual Fold** - `Ctrl+K Ctrl+,`\n- **Go to Named Region** - `Ctrl+Shift+R` ← New!\n\n---\n\n## More Information\n\nFor implementation details and architecture, see:\n- `IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md` - Technical details\n- Source files:\n  - `src/vs/editor/contrib/folding/browser/namedRangeProvider.ts`\n  - `src/vs/editor/contrib/folding/browser/folding.ts`\n  - `src/vs/editor/contrib/folding/test/browser/namedRangeProvider.test.ts`\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

picking:', picks);\n```\n\n### Check Line Numbers\n\nVerify line numbers are 1-indexed:\n```typescript\nconsole.assert(region.startLineNumber >= 1, 'Line number should be 1-indexed');\n```\n\n### Test Region Stack\n\nDebug the region stack logic:\n```typescript\nconsole.log('Region stack:', regionStack);\n```\n\n---\n\n## Troubleshooting Common Issues\n\n### Issue: Command doesn't execute\n**Solution:** Check that:\n- Folding is enabled in editor\n- You're in a text editor (not settings)\n- File has content (not empty)\n\n### Issue: Regions not detected\n**Solution:** Verify:\n- Using exact pattern: `#region` and `#endregion`\n- In comments (not strings or code)\n- Matching pairs (same number of starts/ends)\n\n### Issue: Navigation doesn't work\n**Solution:** Check:\n- Editor has focus\n- Region is valid and in file\n- Line number is correct\n\n### Issue: Dialog doesn't show\n**Solution:**\n- Try `Ctrl+Shift+P` and search for command\n- Check keybinding in Users mark code sections with special comments (e.g., `// #region MySection` / `// #endregion`) and can then quickly jump between them using `Ctrl+Shift+R`.\n\n**Quick Start:**\n```typescript\n// #region Imports\nimport React from 'react';\n// #endregion\n\n// #region Component\nexport function App() {\n  return <div>Hello</div>;\n}\n// #endregion\n```\n\nPress `Ctrl+Shift+R` (or `Cmd+Shift+R` on Mac) to navigate!\n\n---\n\n## 📁 Implementation Files\n\n### Core Implementation\n\n#### 1. **Named Region Provider** (`namedRangeProvider.ts`)\n- **Purpose:** Detect and extract named regions from code\n- **Size:** ~57 lines\n- **Exports:**\n  - `NamedRange` interface\n  - `getNamedRegions(model)` function\n\n#### 2. **Folding Integration** (`folding.ts` - MODIFIED)\n- **Changes:** +48 lines\n- **Additions:**\n  - Imports for `IQuickInputService` and `getNamedRegions`\n  - `GoToNamedRegion` EditorAction class\n  - Action registration\n\n#### 3. **Test Suite** (`namedRangeProvider.test.ts`)\n- **Purpose:** Comprehensive testing of region detection\n- **Size:** ~191 lines\n- **Coverage:** 12 test cases covering all functionality\n\n### Documentation Files\n\n1. **IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md** - Technical architecture and design\n2. **USAGE_GUIDE.md** - User-facing documentation with examples\n3. **REQUIREMENTS_CHECKLIST.md** - Verification of all requirements\n4. **TESTING_GUIDE.md** - Manual and automated testing procedures\n5. **README.md** - This file\n\n---\n\n## ✨ Key Features\n\n### 1. **Automatic Region Detection**\n- Scans code for `#region` / `#endregion` markers\n- Works with any comment style (`//`, `/* */`, etc.)\n- Handles nested regions correctly\n- Line numbers are 1-indexed for VS Code compatibility\n\n### 2. **Quick-Pick Navigation**\n- Press `Ctrl+Shift+R` to open the region navigator\n- Shows all regions with their starting line numbers\n- Type to filter regions by name\n- Select to navigate instantly\n\n### 3. **Smooth Navigation**\n- Cursor moves to region start\n- Viewport scrolls smoothly to show the region\n- Proper focus and selection handling\n\n### 4. **Robust Implementation**\n- Handles edge cases (no regions, unmatched markers, etc.)\n- Works across all supported languages\n- No conflicts with existing folding features\n- Follows VS Code architectural patterns\n\n---\n\n## 🚀 Getting Started\n\n### For Users\n\n1. **Mark Your Regions**\n```typescript\n// #region Section Name\n// ... your code ...\n// #endregion\n```\n\n2. **Navigate**\n   - Press `Ctrl+Shift+R` (Windows/Linux) or `Cmd+Shift+R` (Mac)\n   - Select a region from the quick-pick menu\n   - Navigate to that region instantly\n\n### For Developers\n\n1. **Understanding the Code**\n   - Start with `IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md`\n   - Read `namedRangeProvider.ts` for the core logic\n   - Review `folding.ts` for integration details\n\n2. **Running Tests**\n```bash\nnpm test -- in `folding.ts` to change UI behavior\n   - Add tests to `namedRangeProvider.test.ts`\n\n---\n\n## 📊 Project Statistics\n\n| Metric | Value |\n|--------|-------|\n| **Files Created** | 2 |\n| **Files Algorithm\n```\nFor each line in the text model:\n  ├─ Check if line contains \"#region <name>\"\n  │  └─ If yes, push (name, lineNumber) to stack\n  │\n  └─ Check if line contains \"#endregion\"\n     └─ If yes, pop from stack and record region pair\n```\n\n### Navigation Flow\n```\nUser presses Ctrl+Shift+R\n       ↓\nGet editor's text model\n       ↓\nCall getNamedRegions(model)\n       ↓\nShow quick-pick with regions\n       ↓\nUser selects a region\n       ↓\nSet cursor position to region start\n       ↓\nSmooth scroll to show region\n```\n\n---\n\n## 📚 Documentation\n\n| Document |
